title: Unique Light Irish Potato Paella
description: A rich and aerated Irish dish featuring Thai chilli jam, this paella is a unique blend of flavors and textures, made in a traditional paella pan and emulsified to perfection, it is also Jain-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the diced potatoes, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes are slightly tender.
Add the Thai chilli jam, lemon juice, and vegetable broth, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Use an immersion blender to emulsify the paella, or let it cool and emulsify it in a blender.
Serve the paella hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
